evals.prompt: We've got sp014's solution settled on paper — render the i3 status bar with a nushell script instead of the current bash one. But I'm not actually sure nushell starts up fast enough to redraw the bar without visible lag, and I don't want spec-refinement to plan out a whole task breakdown around an approach that might not hold. Can we confirm this is viable before we go further?
evals.expected_output: Triggers idea-poc as the spec-writing confidence gate. Frames a falsifiable hypothesis (nushell renders the full status line under a latency budget), isolates in a throwaway poc/<slug> worktree or scratch dir, runs the smallest experiment, reads the verdict, records docs/notes/lab/poc###.md via `akm poc write --informs sp014` with a category and verdict status, discards the throwaway, and feeds the recommendation back to spec-writing (validated → cite [[poc###]] in ## solution → spec-refinement). Does NOT start the real i3blocks rewrite.

evals.prompt: sp012's solution leans on streaming uploads through library X. The solution shape looks right but I've never seen library X handle large payloads — I'm not confident enough to hand this to spec-refinement. Should we just go with it?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the confidence gate: the chosen ## solution rests on an unproven assumption (library X streaming at size), so it should NOT be finalized / handed to spec-refinement yet. Branches to idea-poc: hypothesis about library X streaming behavior at size, isolated experiment, poc### verdict (likely surfacing a limit) recorded with --informs sp012. If invalidated, recommends revising the solution (e.g. chunked multipart) and looping a new PoC; if validated, cite [[poc###]] and proceed.

evals.prompt: prove that carapace completions can be lazy-loaded in nushell without breaking the prompt — spike it
evals.expected_output: Direct idea-poc trigger ('prove', 'spike'). Falsifiable hypothesis about lazy-loading carapace, isolated throwaway experiment, honest verdict, poc### record (--informs omitted is acceptable for a standalone spike with no spec yet), throwaway discarded. Should not modify the real nushell config.
